name: Odiel Marshes Greenway
name_original: Desde Corales a la Isla Saltés por las Marismas del Odiel
description: Cycle 20.3 miles (32.7 km) through the easy Odiel Marshes, spotting birds and enjoying estuary views to Saltés Island.
region_intro: No traffic touring cycling routes in Marismas Del Odiel are characterized by extensive wetlands, flat terrain, and a diverse ecosystem at the confluence of the Tinto and Odiel rivers. The region features estuaries, salt flats, and channels separating islands, alongside pine forests and beaches. This landscape provides a setting for touring cycling with minimal elevation changes. Best no traffic touring cycling routes in Marismas Del Odiel The most popular no traffic touring cycling route is Odiel Marshes Greenway, a 20.3 miles (32.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This route offers bird-spotting opportunities and estuary views towards Saltés Island. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Umbría Tower – Chiringuito Oliver loop from Punta Umbría, an easy 6.1 miles (9.9 km) path. This loop provides views of the coastal area around Punta Umbría. Local touring cyclists also love the El Portil Beach – El Portil Lagoon loop from Restaurante Rifeño, a 9.0 miles (14.5 km) trail leading through beaches and lagoons, often completed in about 58 minutes. Touring cycling in Marismas Del Odiel is defined by wetlands, estuaries, and pine forests, with routes offering options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Marismas Del Odiel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 1800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Marismas Del Odiel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic touring cycling routes are available in Marismas Del Odiel?
A: There are over 60 dedicated no-traffic touring cycling routes in Marismas Del Odiel, offering a wide range of options for exploring the natural park's unique landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Marismas Del Odiel?
A: The ideal times for touring cycling in Marismas del Odiel are spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild, and migratory birds are most active, providing excellent birdwatching opportunities.

Q: What kind of terrain and surface can I expect on the no-traffic cycling routes?
A: The routes in Marismas del Odiel are generally flat, making them suitable for all fitness levels. You'll encounter a mix of paved sections and well-maintained dirt paths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987175276/odiel-marshes-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Odiel Marshes Greenway&lt;/a&gt; features both, so a mountain or hybrid bike is often recommended for comfort and versatility.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly no-traffic routes suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, Marismas del Odiel is excellent for families and beginners due to its flat terrain. Many routes are classified as easy, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38257229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Umbría Tower – Chiringuito Oliver loop from Punta Umbría&lt;/a&gt;, which is just under 10 km and offers minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed family outing.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the cycling routes?
A: The routes offer numerous points of interest. You can cycle past historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7426162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tharsis Pier&lt;/a&gt;, explore the unique landscapes of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Odiel Marshes&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4525711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Astur Saltworks&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also provide access to bird observatories and viewpoints for wildlife spotting.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the no-traffic touring cycling trails?
A: While Marismas del Odiel is a natural park, specific rules for dogs on trails can vary. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect the local wildlife, especially in a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. Always check local signage or the park's official guidelines for the most current regulations regarding pets on trails.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Marismas Del Odiel?
A: Yes, many of the no-traffic touring cycling routes in Marismas del Odiel are designed as loops. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14587128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Portil Beach – El Portil Lagoon loop from Restaurante Rifeño&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a convenient way to start and end your ride at the same point while enjoying diverse scenery.

Q: What are the opportunities for birdwatching along the cycling routes?
A: Marismas del Odiel is a prime location for birdwatching, being a Special Protection Area for Birds. Many cycling routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987175276/odiel-marshes-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Odiel Marshes Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, pass directly through key habitats. You can expect to see flamingos, herons, ospreys, and a significant population of European spoonbills, especially during migratory seasons.

Q: Where can I find parking for the no-traffic cycling routes?
A: Parking is typically available near the main access points and visitor centers of the natural park. For routes starting near urban areas like Punta Umbría or Huelva, you'll find designated parking facilities. It's recommended to check specific route descriptions for the most convenient parking options.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Marismas Del Odiel?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Reviewers often praise the tranquil, traffic-free environment, the stunning birdwatching opportunities, and the flat, accessible terrain that makes for enjoyable rides for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any cafes or accommodation options near the cycling trails?
A: While the core of the natural park is preserved, you'll find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation in the nearby towns and coastal areas surrounding Marismas del Odiel, such as Huelva and Punta Umbría. These towns serve as excellent bases for exploring the cycling routes.

Q: Can I access the cycling routes using public transport?
A: Public transport options, primarily buses, connect Huelva with surrounding towns and some areas bordering the natural park. While direct access to every trailhead might be limited, you can often reach a starting point within a reasonable cycling distance from a bus stop. Check local bus schedules for routes serving the Marismas del Odiel area.
tips: It is a natural area (wetland) located at the mouth of the Odiel river or Huelva estuary. It constitutes one of the largest extensions of marshes in Spain. There are several trails and bike routes that allow you to observe nature (for example, the flamingo, the spoonbill, the heron and the osprey).

We are in the middle of the mouth of the Odiel river, between the three islands of the Marismas del Odiel Natural Area, which was declared a Biosphere Reserve by UNESCO and a Special Protection Area for Birds and is considered one of the most important tidal marshes in the Iberian Peninsula. Enmedio Island stands out for the excellent degree of conservation of its ecosystem. On Isla Saltes you can frequently see the chameleon.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the 'From Corales to Saltés Island through the Odiel Marshes' route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route usually takes around 1 hour and 49 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied terrain, combining paved paths with dirt trails as it winds through salt flats, pine groves, and wetlands. You'll experience a gentle ascent, reaching a maximum of 40 meters above sea level.

faqs.question: What type of bike is recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: Due to the unpaved sections and varied terrain, a mountain bike or hybrid bike is recommended for this route. Road bikes may find some parts challenging.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and picturesque scenery, including extensive marshes, estuaries, and pine forests. It's a fantastic spot for birdwatching, with opportunities to observe flamingos, spoonbills, herons, and ospreys. The air often carries the scent of the sea and native marsh vegetation.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers panoramic views of the estuary and Isla Saltés. A significant highlight is crossing the iconic Bridge between Isla de Bacuta Sur and Isla Saltés. You'll also traverse the beautiful Odiel Marshes, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the Marismas del Odiel Natural Site?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the Marismas del Odiel Natural Site, which is a protected area. Generally, there are no entrance fees for accessing the natural site itself for recreational activities like cycling. However, it's always a good idea to check local regulations for any specific activities or areas within the reserve. You can find more information on the official tourism website for Spain: spain.info.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Marismas del Odiel Natural Site is a vital habitat for migratory birds, making spring and autumn excellent times for birdwatching. These seasons also offer milder temperatures, which are ideal for cycling. Summer can be quite hot, while winter might bring cooler, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Marismas del Odiel Natural Site is a protected area with sensitive wildlife, dogs are generally permitted on trails if kept on a leash. Please ensure you clean up after your pet and respect the natural environment and other visitors.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the starting point in Corales is not detailed, but access points to the Marismas del Odiel Natural Site typically have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check local maps or visitor center information for the most convenient and official parking near the Corales starting point.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Carril bici La Calatilla, Calatilla de Bacuta, Pasarela 1, Pasarela 2, Pasarela 3, and the Camino Verde Huelva - Punta Umbría VM-497, offering a diverse cycling experience.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered easy, making it suitable for beginner to intermediate cyclists and those with all fitness levels. It focuses on accessibility and enjoying nature rather than challenging or technical riding.

waypoints.name: Bridge between Isla de Bacuta Sur and Isla Saltés
